Magical Overrides
Override Cap raises the 20 cap for additive bonuses (e.g. an item granting +2 WIS beyond 20). Hard Set forces an ability to a fixed value, ignoring all other calculations (e.g. Belt of Giant Strength).

🎲 Add Mutation ✕
Roll d100 at the table, then enter the result here to look it up.
Enter a result above to see the mutation.
Contamination ✕
Monsters of Drakkenheim, p374 — homebrew

While you have Contamination, you experience the following effects.

Contamination Levels. This condition is cumulative. Each time you receive it, you gain one or more levels of Contamination. You undergo a Monstrous Transformation if your Contamination level reaches 6.

Contamination Symptoms. A Contaminated creature suffers the symptoms from its current level as well as all lower levels. Conditions gained from Contamination end when those Contamination levels end. There is no other way to remove conditions caused by symptoms of Contamination.

LvSymptoms
1None
2Halve the Hit Points you regain when you expend Hit Dice during a Short Rest
3You do not regain Hit Points when you finish a Long Rest
4You have the Weakened condition
5You have the Dazed condition
6Monstrous Transformation!

Mutations. Each time you gain a Contamination level, roll 1d6. If the result is equal to or lower than your current Contamination level, you gain a randomly determined mutation by rolling on the Mutations table. The effects of the mutation remain while you have Contamination levels. Unless otherwise specified, a creature can't gain the same mutation more than once. If a duplicate result is rolled, the GM chooses a different one or rolls again.

Long Rests. Each time you finish a Long Rest while you have 1 or more Contamination levels, roll 1d20. On a 1, you gain a Contamination level.

Removing Contamination Levels. Only specific magic can remove Contamination levels:

  • The Purge Contamination spell.
  • Heal removes all Contamination levels and mutations in addition to its other effects.

Contamination is not a curse, poison, or disease. Spells such as Lesser Restoration, Protection from Poison, and Remove Curse do not remove Contamination levels.

Being raised from the dead reduces a creature's Contamination level by 1.

When your Contamination level reaches 0, the condition ends, as well as the effects of all mutations.

Skin, hair, fingernails, and toenails lost to mutations regrow normally once Contamination is removed. However, a Regenerate spell or similar magic is needed to restore any other body parts (such as teeth, limbs, or eyes) lost due to Contamination. Appendages or limbs that develop as a result of mutations wither and fall off when Contamination is removed, and other warped body parts are restored to their original form.

Dying with Contamination Levels. If you die while you have one or more Contamination levels, your corpse reanimates as a Haze Husk 24 hours later. A creature with six or more class levels or hit dice rises as a Haze Wight instead.

Monstrous Transformation! If you end your turn with six or more Contamination Levels, you permanently transform into a horrific monster controlled by the Game Master.

The Game Master chooses the new form, which is most often an Aberration or Monstrosity of some kind such as a Gibbering Mouther, Delerium Dreg, or Pyknic Maunder.

Your game statistics are entirely replaced by those of the chosen monster, including the new form's Hit Points and Hit Dice.

All Contamination levels are removed, though the GM may grant additional traits to the new form similar to any beneficial mutations gained from Contamination Levels.

The Game Master determines what remains of the original creature's personality and memories, if anything. Regardless, the creature falls under the Game Master's control.

Reversing the Transformation. A Wish spell or similar magic can restore a single transformed creature to its previous form, removing all Contamination and restoring the original creature's personality and memories. This is considered a stressful use of the Wish spell, and thus there is a 33 percent chance that the caster will be unable to cast Wish ever again if they use the spell in this manner.

Note that the means of reversing a monstrous transformation are completely unknown at the outset of the campaign. A Monstrous Transformation is widely regarded as permanent.

✦ Grant Free Spell ✕
Adds a spell to your known list without it counting against your known/prepared limit — for DM rewards, story items, or anything not covered by an automatic source (invocations, feats, subclass features, etc).
✦ Add Custom Spell ✕
Homebrew spells this character knows that aren't in the standard catalog. Once saved, find it in Browse Spells like any other spell to add it to your known list.

Bonuses (leave 0 for none)
The Weapon/Armor/Shield bonus only does something once you check the matching box above and link/equip the item — same as catalog items.
Granted Spells
For spells the item lets you cast without a slot — e.g. "Wand of Fireballs: 3/day". Limited-use grants get their own entry in the Resources tracker, separate from the item's own charges above.
Just a reminder for yourself — this doesn't reset automatically, you'll still clear it manually when it's actually due.
This creates a linked entry in your Resources tracker with the usual pip-counter and rest-recovery — that's where you'll mark charges as used.

Level Plan ✕
Assign each level to a class. Level 1 is always your primary class. Subclasses unlock at level 3 of that class.
